_comment: DRAFT 2 (batch 2) - official English/romanized names for non-China venues with no Latin name in venues_catalog.json (Taiwan, Russia, Japan, Greece, Hungary, Italy). Not merged into any live data file. Generated 2026-07-03. Hall/room suffixes (演藝廳/大ホール/實驗劇場/多功能廳 etc.) intentionally stripped from 'en' where the parent building has an official English name. 'native' matches the venues_catalog.json name field exactly (including doubled strings). Low-confidence entries left with empty 'en' (no invented transliteration).

venues.native: 介壽堂 馬祖南竿介壽堂
venues.note: Historic 1970s cinema/hall in Jieshou Village, Nangan. No official English name found on Lienchiang County or any government English source; left empty per no-invent rule.

venues.native: 台北生技園區多功能廳
venues.note: Stripped 多功能廳 (Multipurpose Hall) to building level. Operator's official English site uses 'Taipei Bioinnovation Park'.

venues.native: 國立陽明交通大學 演藝廳 國立陽明交通大學 演藝廳
venues.note: University's official arts English page names 演藝廳 'Music Performance Hall'; NYCU is the official English abbreviation.

venues.native: 國立陽明交通大學光復校區大禮堂
venues.note: Guangfu Campus grand auditorium (大禮堂); no official English name published, left empty.
Run by Quanta Arts Foundation; foundation's own verified page names it '廣藝廳 (Quanta Hall)'.

venues.native: 彰化演藝廳 彰化縣政府文化局 演藝廳
venues.note: Auditorium on 1F of Changhua County Cultural Affairs Bureau; Bureau's English site lists no English name, left empty.

venues.native: 新竹縣政府文化局演藝廳
venues.note: Named 'Hsinchu County Performance Hall' on the Cultural Affairs Bureau of Hsinchu County Government's official English site.

venues.native: 樹林藝文中心（演藝廳） 樹林藝文中心 演藝廳
venues.note: Stripped 演藝廳 (Performance Hall) to building level. 'Shulin Arts Center' per New Taipei Culture Bureau's official English site.
Under Penghu County Cultural Affairs Bureau; consistent English rendering (official FB handle penghuperformancehall), no formal gov English page.

venues.native: 空軍三重一村時藝劇場
venues.note: Black-box theatre in Air Force Sanchong First Village, branded by operator MediaSphere. No official English name published for 時藝劇場; left empty.

venues.native: 臺中市屯區藝文中心實驗劇場
venues.en: Taichung City Tun District Art Center
venues.note: Stripped 實驗劇場 (Experimental Theater) to building level. Name per its own official English site.

venues.native: 臺北市政府親子劇場 臺北市政府親子劇場
venues.note: Venue at Taipei City Hall 2F, run by Taipei Arts Promotion Office. Direct translation of 親子劇場; exact venue-name English not fully settled.

venues.native: 臺東藝文中心 臺東縣政府文化處藝文中心 演藝廳
venues.note: Stripped 演藝廳 (Performance Hall) to building level. Name per Taitung County Cultural Affairs Dept. official material.

venues.native: 臺灣大學藝文中心遊心劇場
venues.note: NTU's own naming: official article states the English name of 遊心劇場 is 'University Players Theatre'.

venues.native: 萬座曉劇場 萬座曉劇場
venues.note: Wanhua venue run by Shinehouse Theatre (曉劇場); own official FB names it '萬座曉劇場 Wan Theater'.
'eslite Performance Hall' on eslite's own official English page (Songshan Cultural and Creative Park).
Shared/event space; self-identified official English 'Project Nomad' (handle projectnomad.taipei), no formal institutional page.
Yunlin County Cultural Affairs Dept. venue; 'Yunlin Performance Hall' used consistently by county official channels.
The 正港小劇場 at Pier-2 is officially 'Kaohsiung Experimental Theatre' (KET) per Pier-2's English site.

venues.native: 高雄市立社會教育館 高雄市社教館 演藝廳
venues.note: Stripped 演藝廳 (Performance Hall) to building level. Name per the institution's own official English site.

venues.native: Академический Малый драматический театр – Театр Европы Академический Малый драматический театр – Театр Европы
venues.en: Maly Drama Theatre – Theatre of Europe
venues.note: English Wikipedia title 'Maly Drama Theatre'; own English site renders full 'Maly Drama Theatre – Theatre of Europe'. Native string doubled.

venues.native: Большой драматический театр имени Г. А. Товстоногова Большой драматический театр имени Г. А. Товстоногова
venues.note: English Wikipedia article title; named after Georgy Tovstonogov, aka BDT. Native string doubled.

venues.native: Большой театр. Камерная сцена им.Покровского Большой театр. Камерная сцена им.Покровского
venues.en: Bolshoi Theatre – Boris Pokrovsky Chamber Stage
venues.note: Former Pokrovsky Chamber Opera merged into Bolshoi (2017); Operabase/Bolshoi English render 'Boris Pokrovsky Chamber Stage of the Bolshoi Theatre'. Native string doubled.

venues.native: Государственный Академический Театр имени Евгения Вахтангова
venues.note: Exact English Wikipedia article title; official site vakhtangov.ru/en.
Municipal House of Culture (ДК); no official English name and no English Wikipedia article. Left empty.
Local Actors' House / Union of Theatre Workers venue; no official English name. Native string doubled. Left empty.

venues.native: Дом культуры и творчества им. В.П. Чкалова
venues.note: House of Culture named after V.P. Chkalov; no official English name, only auto-translations. Left empty.

venues.native: Камерный Театр Объединенного Музея Писателей Урала Камерный Театр Объединенного Музея Писателей Урала
venues.note: Chamber Theatre of the United Museum of Ural Writers; only Russian sources, no official English name. Native string doubled. Left empty.
Concert hall near Finland Station; official site Russian-only, no established English name. Left empty.
No English Wikipedia article, but theatre's own domain self-renders as 'Modern Theatre' (Art Nouveau/Modern style). Also 'Moscow Drama Theatre Modern'.
Exact English Wikipedia article title; world's oldest Romani theatre, founded 1931.
Renamed from 'Theatre on Malaya Bronnaya' to 'Theatre on Bronnaya' (2021); mos.ru English renders 'Theatre on (Malaya) Bronnaya'. No standalone English Wikipedia article.

venues.en: Central Academic Theatre of the Russian Army
venues.note: Wikidata English label matches native literally; English Wikipedia article under short title 'Russian Army Theatre'.
Exact English Wikipedia article title; official site uses 'Stanislavsky Electrotheatre'. Member of Union of Theatres of Europe.
Official site gives no English name; 'Aiplaza Toyohashi' is the established romanization (also on venue Facebook). aka 豊橋勤労福祉会館.

venues.native: アクリエひめじ(姫路市文化コンベンションセンター)
venues.note: Building-level official English (JNTO + operator). Nickname アクリエ has no official romanization; returned official building name.

venues.native: サンケイホールブリーゼ
venues.note: Official English used by Osaka Convention Bureau and builder Kajima.
No official English site; 'Theater H' used consistently by NAVITIME. Shinagawa-ku.
Official site Japanese-only; 'Theater Sunmall' (also 'Theater Sun-mall') consistent romanization. Shinjuku.

venues.native: パルテノン多摩 大ホール
venues.note: Stripped 大ホール (Large Hall); 'Parthenon Tama' is the building's official Latin-script name.

venues.native: ヒューリックホール東京
venues.note: Official English name (operator Hulic). Yurakucho, Chiyoda-ku.

venues.native: 刈谷市総合文化センター アイリス 大ホール
venues.note: Stripped 大ホール (Large Hall); 'Kariya Cultural Center Iris' per Tokyo Art Beat. No English on official site.
Official English on venue site: 'THE KURE SHINKIN BANK HALL'. Naming-rights name of 呉市文化ホール.

venues.native: 土岐市文化プラザ サンホール
venues.note: No official English found (site JP-only). Building ~ Toki City Cultural Plaza, hall = サンホール (Sun Hall); no established English, left empty.
Official English (asahi-hall.jp). 11F Yurakucho Mullion, Chiyoda-ku.

venues.native: 福岡市民ホール 大ホール
venues.note: Stripped 大ホール (Main Hall); official English building name 'Fukuoka Civic Hall'. Opened 2025, successor to 福岡市民会館.
Venue's own name is 'WE' (native doubles it); multipurpose venue in a former military warehouse. Latin-script proper name kept.
Standard transliteration/translation; open-air municipal theatre in Sykies named after actor Manos Katrakis. No official English name.
Transliterated 'Theatro T' by TripAdvisor; kept romanized proper name.
Standard transliteration; theatre in Plaka. No official English rendering on site.

venues.native: Θέατρο Τεχνών Θεσσαλονίκης Θέατρο Τεχνών Θεσσαλονίκης
venues.note: Native string doubled. Standard translation; 250-seat venue in Faliro, Thessaloniki.
Named after actress Jenny (Tzeni) Karezi; venue at Akademias 3, Athens.
Established English name; historic 1935 open-air cinema opposite the Acropolis, per official Athens guide.
Native string doubled. 'Μικρή Σκηνή' = 'Small Stage'; music/theatre venue in Kato Toumpa.
Official English name (own site + English Wikipedia). Oldest performing-arts institution in modern Greece, founded 1871.
Official English name 'A38 Ship' (Hungarian 'A38 Hajó'). Concert hall on a reconstructed ship on the Danube.
Already Latin-script; 'Dóm tér' = 'Cathedral Square'. Open-air square hosting the Szeged Open-Air Festival (~4,000 seats).
Already Latin-script; official name 'Teatro Q77' (also 'Q77 Vertigo Live'). 200-seat multipurpose theatre at Corso Brescia 77, Turin.
